chunks.text: November 2011 What if you are threatened in a lab? Hello students, instructors and staff: The shooting in a computer lab (http://newscenter.berkeley.edu/2011/11/15/) at Haas School of

enter.berkeley.edu/2011/11/15/) at Haas School of Business on Nov 15, 2011 was a serious concern for us all. Ways to call for help during an emergency in one of our labs: * from a campus phone: call 911 (UCPD) * from a cell phone: call 91

call 911 (UCPD) * from a cell phone: call 911 (CA Highway Patrol) * from a cell phone: call 510-642-3333 (UCPD) * from a cell phone: text 510-664-8477 (UCPD) * email cal@tipnow.com (http://police.berkeley.edu/caltip/) (UCPD) Acce

(http://police.berkeley.edu/caltip/) (UCPD) Access to every EECS instructional lab is restricted by cardkey on evenings, weekends and holidays. Many labs are restricted 24x7. Students in EECS classes are automatically enabled for cardkey access

asses are automatically enabled for cardkey access to the labs for their classes. (This is a good time to remember the rule not to let unauthorized people into the buildings or labs.) The UC Police Department has posted an informative video to

ice Department has posted an informative video to help us be prepared in the event of a shooter or other threat in our confined lab spaces: "Shots Fired" (https://bspace.berkeley.edu/portal/page/4bd38df7-ea22-47ac-881c-9fabd3565388) (Login to bSpac

38df7-ea22-47ac-881c-9fabd3565388) (Login to bSpace. The video is in Flash.) To be automatically notified of emergencies, you can subscribe to: * http://warnme.berkeley.edu/ (UC Berkeley) * http://www.ci.berkeley.ca.us/ContentDisplay.aspx?id=2

p://www.ci.berkeley.ca.us/ContentDisplay.aspx?id=25416 (City of Berkeley) * https://alertsf.org/, http://twitter.com/alertsf (City of San Francicso) Please report any observations or concerns you have about safety in our labs. You can report to

ave about safety in our labs. You can report to EECS staff in any of these ways: * visit 251 Cory or 387 Soda (main dept offices) * visit 377/378/380/384/386 Cory or 333 Soda (instructional support) * email inst@eecs.berkeley.edu * call 51
port) * email inst@eecs.berkeley.edu * call 510-643-6141 Thank you, Kevin Mullally Manager, EECS Instructional Support Group http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~kevinm
